hub_subtitle: From hesitant communicator to confident global business leader

lesson.menu_title: After the Deal — Delivery & Keeping the Client
lesson.menu_desc: Run everything after the handshake: confirm the invoice, track the shipment, hit each milestone, handle after-sales and onboard a client to keep — and talk about what WILL happen and what you ARE going to do with future forms (will, be going to, present continuous for arrangements) — 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 — After the Deal — Delivery & Keeping the Client
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Milton Sayegh | Aula 14 — After the Deal | Business English for Luxury Export | Alumni by Better

listenings.text: Hi Milton, it is Karen from Halstead, just confirming the next steps after we signed. I am going to send the signed paperwork over to you this afternoon, and the invoice will follow by email tomorrow. On our side, the lead time you quoted works well, so we are expecting the first shipment in about three weeks. I will track it on our end, and I am going to flag any delay early so it does not surprise anyone. One small thing: our store opening is the big milestone for us, so as long as the deliverable lands a few days before, we are in great shape. I am really glad we settled this, and I think this is the start of something we are both going to be happy with. Talk soon.

Hey team, Milton here with a quick voice note now that the Halstead deal is signed. The hard part is over, but the work that keeps the client is just starting. First, I am going to send the invoice and the paperwork today, so nothing stalls. The lead time is three weeks, and we are shipping the first batch on schedule, so let us protect that date, because their store opening is the milestone that matters to them. After delivery, I want our after-sales to be flawless: I will call Karen myself a week in, and we are going to onboard her team on how to care for the pieces. If we fulfill every promise on this first order, we retain them for years, not one season. Long-term clients are the whole game, so let us treat the paperwork and the follow-up as seriously as we treated the deal. Thanks, everyone.
